full_prompt: Design a continuous-carbon-fiber-reinforced chopped-CF-nylon 3D-printed replacement wheel bracket for a light tactical vehicle per a canonical AM composite case-study template, sized to survive a rated load of 14,000 lbf (62.3 kN) at the bolted wheel-hub interface with a composite-structure safety factor of 2.0 on ultimate. The bracket attaches a steering linkage pickup to the axle assembly and must fit within an envelope of 180 mm x 120 mm x 80 mm. Interfaces: one 16 mm diameter through-hole for the axle mounting stud on the top face, two 12 mm diameter through-holes for the linkage clevis pin on the front face (centered on a 40 mm spacing). Material: chopped-carbon-fiber-reinforced nylon matrix (nylon 6 + chopped CF) with continuous carbon fiber (CCF) layers printed concentrically around stress paths and as floor/roof reinforcement. Published properties for CCF-reinforced CF-nylon (per CCF-AM material data sheet 2024): flexural strength (axial CF) ~= 540 MPa, tensile strength (axial CF) ~= 800 MPa, Young's modulus (axial) ~= 60 GPa, density ~= 1.4 g/cm^3; transverse (off-fiber) properties significantly lower (typically 50-80 MPa tensile). Load cases, derived from the canonical AM composite case study + military vehicle service: LC1 rated pull 62.3 kN (14,000 lbf) along the linkage pin axis; LC2 vertical axle load 20 kN applied at the 16 mm hole; LC3 combined LC1 + LC2 at 75% each (46.7 kN + 15 kN); LC4 20 g impact multiplier on LC1 (for rough-terrain shock), amplifying LC1 to ~65 kN peak but limited to composite ultimate by the primary pass/fail. Pass/fail: (R1) under LC1, max von Mises (isotropic approximation) <= 800 / 2.0 = 400 MPa along reinforcement direction and <= 50 / 2.0 = 25 MPa transverse to reinforcement, enforced via anisotropic composite failure criterion (Tsai-Wu or Hashin); (R2) max displacement at the linkage pin under LC1 <= 2 mm (steering tolerance requirement typical for light tactical vehicles); (R3) bracket mass <= 800 g (canonical AM composite case study reported ~680 g vs ~3 kg original steel); (R4) combined LC3 failure index (Tsai-Wu) <= 1/1.5 = 0.67 (SF 1.5 on composite interaction); (R5) bolt bearing stress at 16 mm hole on chopped-CF-nylon matrix <= 100 MPa (composite bearing allowable with 2.0 SF against 200 MPa ultimate); (R6) manufacturability: CCF layer count >= 4 per 5 mm of wall thickness; printed wall must have at least 2 chopped-CF-nylon floor/roof layers; build orientation must place continuous CF layers parallel to principal load direction. Deliverables: STEP AP242 + AM-slicer-compatible STL, with a layer plan (or reference to one) specifying CCF routing; report with Tsai-Wu failure index per load case, pin deflection, bracket mass, bearing stress, and CCF-layer routing description.
short_prompt:
Design a continuous-carbon-fiber-reinforced chopped-CF-nylon 3D-printed replacement wheel bracket for a light tactical vehicle, sized to survive a rated 14,000 lbf (62.3 kN) load at the bolted wheel-hub interface with a composite-structure safety factor of 2.0 on ultimate.

Envelope: 180 x 120 x 80 mm. Interfaces: one 16 mm through-hole for the axle mounting stud on the top face, two 12 mm through-holes for the linkage clevis pin on the front face (40 mm spacing).

Material: nylon 6 + chopped CF matrix with concentric continuous-CF layers around stress paths and as floor/roof reinforcement. Published properties (CCF-AM 2024 data sheet): flexural strength (axial CF) ~540 MPa, tensile (axial CF) ~800 MPa, Young's modulus ~60 GPa, density ~1.4 g/cm^3; transverse tensile typically 50-80 MPa.

Load cases.
- LC1 rated pull 62.3 kN (14,000 lbf) along the linkage pin axis.
- LC2 vertical axle load 20 kN at the 16 mm hole.
- LC3 combined LC1 + LC2 at 75% each (46.7 kN + 15 kN).
- LC4 20 g impact multiplier on LC1 (rough-terrain shock), peak ~65 kN limited by composite ultimate.

Pin-hole deflection under LC1 <= 2 mm. Mass <= 800 g. Manufacturability: CCF layer count >= 4 per 5 mm wall, >= 2 chopped-CF floor/roof layers, continuous CF parallel to principal load direction.

requires_non_fea_solver.reason: CCF routing is a build-prep step, not FEA.
Closed-form anisotropic failure check, post-processes FEA stresses.

prompt.title: CCF-reinforced CF-nylon replacement light-tactical-vehicle linkage bracket (14,000 lbf)
prompt.brief: Design a CCF-reinforced chopped-CF-nylon light-tactical-vehicle steering linkage bracket in a 180x120x80 mm envelope rated to 62.3 kN with SF 2.0 on ultimate and Tsai-Wu check.

boundary_conditions.mounting_interface: fully constrained at axle stud boss

pass_fail_criteria.derivation: Canonical AM composite case study target (~680 g achieved)
Minimum fiber density for structural composite AM parts

requirements.governing_standard: CCF-AM Composite Material Data Sheet 2024 + MIL-PRF-32565 (ballistic composite analog, reference only) + ASTM D3518 (in-plane shear properties)

recommended_fea_setup.element_type: layered shell or solid-shell with CCF anisotropic

exclusions_explained.vibration_analysis: Static ratings only per the canonical AM composite case study.
exclusions_explained.thermal_analysis: Ambient service; chopped-CF-nylon glass transition ~145 C not engaged.

notes.numeric_source: CCF-AM Composite Material Data Sheet 2024 (CCF-nylon tensile 800 MPa axial); canonical AM composite light-tactical-vehicle case study 14,000 lbf rating.
